task_completion.description: Agent enters the manager PIN to access the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A login screen or keypad in Floreant POS showing the entry of PIN '1111', or the subsequent manager/main dashboard screen indicating successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to and opens the cash payout function.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box, window, or screen titled 'Pay Out', 'Cash Payout', or similar within the Floreant POS interface.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the correct payout amount.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value '12.50' is visible in the amount input field of the payout dialog.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the correct reason for the payout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Emergency Limes' is visible in the reason, note, or description input field of the payout dialog.

task_completion.description: Agent confirms and saves the payout transaction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicking an 'OK', 'Save', 'Pay', or 'Confirm' button on the payout dialog, or a subsequent success message/receipt confirming the payout was recorded.

integrity.description: Agent used the Floreant POS graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Floreant POS GUI buttons and text fields, rather than terminal windows executing commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Floreant POS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window displays Floreant POS branding and standard point-of-sale UI elements, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI by directly modifying the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of database management tools (like DBeaver or command-line SQL clients) being used to insert the PAY_OUT record directly into the Apache Derby database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Floreant POS uses '1111' as the default manager PIN. The system natively supports a 'PAY_OUT' transaction type, which is typically accessed via the manager or back-office interface to record cash leaving the drawer.
